---
title: "Adonis Pooling test"
output: html_notebook
---

The goal of this notebook is to assemble a reference beta diveristy data set
with multiple imputed adonis results that can be imported into python for 
multiple imputation because I dont hate myself enough to try and implement 
the pooled coeffecient in R.

To generate this notebook, I used data from a study by Vangay et al, whcih looked at immigration.

```{r}
library(mice)
library(tidyverse)
library(vegan)
```
We'll start by loading the metadata 

```{r}
meta <- read.table('~/Documents/06.Teaching/datasets/studies/thai_immigration/data2/qiime2_results/qiita-metadata.tsv',
                   sep='\t', 
                   colClasses = 'character',
                   header = TRUE
                   ) %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  mutate_all(., ~replace(., . == '', NA)) %>%
  mutate(., sample.id = paste('12080', sample.id, sep='.')
          , age = as.numeric(age)
          , age_at_arrival = as.numeric(age_at_arrival)
          , years_in_us = as.numeric(years_in_us)
          ) %>%
  filter(., sample_group != 'Control') %>%
  filter(., !is.na(alcohol_use))
```

```{r}
meta %>% 
  mutate_all(., ~is.na(.) * 1) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), sum)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols=everything()) %>%
  arrange(value)
```
```{r}
set.seed(1234)
sub.list <- sample(meta$sample.id, replace = FALSE, size = 250)
sub.meta <- meta %>%
  filter(., sample.id %in% sub.list) %>%
  select(c(sample.id, age, ethnicity, bmi_class, alcohol_use, birth_country)) %>%
  mutate(., alcohol_use = replace(alcohol_use, alcohol_use == 'Daily', 'Weekly')) %>%
  mutate(., alcohol_use = replace(alcohol_use, alcohol_use == 'Quit', 'Never'))
```

And then we'll load the distance matrix

```{r}
# Reads in the distance matrix and 
dm.df <- read.table('~/Documents/06.Teaching/datasets/studies/thai_immigration/data2/qiime2_results/braycurtis_unrarefied.tsv',
                 sep='\t',
                 row.names = 1,
                 header = TRUE)
colnames(dm.df) <- row.names(dm.df)
dm.df <- dm.df[sub.meta$sample.id, sub.meta$sample.id]

dm.dist <- as.dist(dm.df)
```

# Reference fit

I want to start by fitting a simple model that doesnt have any imputation in it
and seeing waht numbers we get without imputation

```{r}
no.imp <- adonis2(dm.dist ~ age + ethnicity + bmi_class + alcohol_use + birth_country, data=sub.meta)
no.imp
```

```{r}
summarize.adonis <- function(res){
  summary <- data.frame(name = attributes(res)[['row.names']],
                        df = res$Df,
                        SumOfSquares=res$SumOfSqs,
                        r2 = res$R2,
                        stat = res$F,
                        pval = res$`Pr(>F)`,
                        var_order = 1:length(res$SumOfSqs)) %>%
    mutate(., var_order = replace(var_order, name %in% c('Total'), NA))
  return(summary)
}
summarize.adonis(no.imp)
```

And then we'll try removing observations from the metadata for MICE. I want to 
start fairly conservatively, so maybe let's pull out 5% of the observations
from everything expect sample ID.

```{r}
meta.miss.amp <- sub.meta %>%
  column_to_rownames('sample.id') %>%
  mutate(., ethnicity = (ethnicity == 'Karen') * 1
          , bmi_class = case_when(bmi_class %in% c('Normal') ~ 0,
                                  bmi_class %in% c('Overweight') ~ 1,
                                  bmi_class %in% c('Obese') ~ 2)
          , alcohol_use = case_when(alcohol_use == 'Never' ~ 0,
                                    alcohol_use == 'Monthly' ~ 1,
                                    alcohol_use == 'Occasionally' ~ 2,
                                    alcohol_use == 'Weekly' ~ 3)
         , birth_country = case_when(birth_country == 'Mynmar' ~ 0,
                                     birth_country == 'Thailand' ~ 1,
                                     birth_country == 'Laos' ~ 2,
                                     birth_country == 'USA' ~ 3)
          ) %>%
  ampute(., prop=0.1)

meta.miss <- meta.miss.amp[['amp']] %>% 
#  rownames_to_column('sample.id') %>%
    mutate(., ethnicity = as.factor(ethnicity)
            , bmi_class = as.factor(bmi_class)
            , alcohol_use = as.factor(alcohol_use)
            , birth_country = as.factor(birth_country))
```

Let's check the imputation worked

```{r}
meta.miss %>%
  mutate_all(., ~is.na(.)) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), sum)) %>%
  pivot_longer(., cols=everything())
```

Now we're going to generate a series of imputed data sets using MICE.

```{r}
mice.imp <- mice(meta.miss, m=10, printFlag = FALSE)
mice.imp
```
And then we can pull off the list of data sets.
```{r}
mice.complete <- complete(mice.imp, action = "all", include = FALSE)
```

And then we'll run the adonis on each model and summarize it.

```{r}
adonis.res <- list()
adonis.summary <- list()
for(i in 1:length(mice.complete)){
  print(i)
  res <- no.imp <- adonis2(dm.dist ~ age + ethnicity + bmi_class + alcohol_use + birth_country,
                           data=data.frame(mice.complete[[i]]))
  summary <- summarize.adonis(res)
  summary$imputation <- i
  adonis.res[[i]] <- res
  adonis.summary[[i]] <- summary
}
```

```{r}
pooled.summary <- do.call(bind_rows, adonis.summary)
write.table(pooled.summary, 
            '~/Repositories/eurydice/eurydice/impute/tests/test_res.tsv',
            sep='\t',
            quote=FALSE)
write.table(summarize.adonis(no.imp),
            '~/Repositories/eurydice/eurydice/impute/tests/no_impute_res.tsv',
            sep='\t',
            quote=FALSE)
```
